pedagogies.name: Visualization
pedagogies.description: Uses visual tools, diagrams, flowcharts, graphs, visual trace of execution, or animations to represent abstract structures and steps.

pedagogies.name: Interactive Lecture
pedagogies.description: Traditional lecturing interspersed with brief discussion, questioning, concept tests, or think-pair-share tasks to keep students engaged.

pedagogies.name: Problem-Based Learning
pedagogies.description: Students solve open-ended, complex, real-world problems to learn principles, algorithm designs, and application details.

pedagogies.name: Project-Based Learning
pedagogies.description: Extended inquiry process structured around complex, authentic questions and carefully designed products or software systems.

pedagogies.name: Inquiry-Based Learning
pedagogies.description: Students pose questions, investigate scenarios, and construct answers/conclusions through exploration.

pedagogies.name: Collaborative Learning
pedagogies.description: Peer instruction, group discussions, and structured group activities to collaborate on solving concepts or design tasks.

pedagogies.name: Simulation & Role Playing
pedagogies.description: Interactive environments or physical/dry-run scenarios where students play parts or act out execution details (e.g. queue enqueue/dequeue steps in BFS).

pedagogies.name: Laboratory Practice
pedagogies.description: Hands-on implementation, coding tasks, setting up configurations, running test cases, or experimentation in a programming lab.

pedagogies.name: Case Study
pedagogies.description: In-depth investigation of a single or group of real-world scenarios to understand practical implications and design choices.

pedagogies.name: Flipped Classroom
pedagogies.description: Students watch lectures or read materials before class, and use class time for active problem solving, Q&A, and discussions.
